title: payment — TenancyHook + lifecycle hooks
description: Cross-module hook implementations. TenancyHooks are the tenancy module's contract for reacting to tenant-lifecycle events (provision / suspend / erase). Payment registers hooks for tenant erasure cascade + user erasure redaction + tenant suspension cascade + invoice-driven intent creation + payment-captured side effects + dispute escalation. No bootstrapper — payment does not need per-request tenant hydration.

hooks.name: PurgePaymentDataForErasedTenant
hooks.purpose: Cascade delete via FK CASCADE across the four payment tables. Writes the compliance audit trail entry + emits count metrics per entity family (payments / payment_methods / payment_intents / payment_disputes purged) + fires best-effort provider-side deletion requests (Stripe Customer.delete, Paddle customer deletion, Adyen shopper deletion, Square customer deletion, PayPal customer deletion) so the propagation is transparent to auditors.
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 §3 — financial-record archival preserves the compliance evidence. Payment records archived to compliance service before hard-delete. AML retention preserved via archive.

hooks.name: ArchivePaymentFinancialRecordsOnTenantErasure
hooks.purpose: Runs BEFORE PurgePaymentDataForErasedTenant (lower priority number). Archives every Payment + PaymentDispute row to the compliance module's financial-record archive (separate storage; regulator-facing). Preserves the immutable financial evidence without keeping the tenant's identifying details.
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 §3 exception — legal obligation to retain financial records supersedes erasure right. National tax law (7y baseline; 10y Enterprise). AML transaction record retention.

hooks.name: RedactCustomerFromPaymentsOnUserErased
hooks.purpose: Individual GDPR Art. 17. Nulls billing_details_snapshot.name + email + phone on payments where the customer resolved to the erased user. Also nulls PaymentMethod.customer_id on methods owned by the user. Payment rows themselves survive (financial retention).
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 §3 — subject-identifying fields redacted; financial rows preserved. Provider-side deletion requests dispatched (best-effort).

hooks.name: DeactivatePaymentMethodsOnUserErased
hooks.purpose: Soft-deletes every PaymentMethod owned by the erased user. Dispatches provider-side detach. Prevents future captures against the erased user's stored tokens.

hooks.name: CreatePaymentIntentOnInvoiceFinalized
hooks.purpose: The primary bridge from invoice → payment. Creates a PaymentIntent for the finalized invoice using the tenant's default provider config + the customer's default PaymentMethod (if any). Skipped when invoice.total_cents = 0.

hooks.name: MarkInvoicePaidOnPaymentCaptured
hooks.purpose: Runs in the same DB transaction as the Payment insert (via observer.creating running inside the wrapping transaction). Updates parent Invoice.status → 'paid' (full amount) or 'partially_paid' (partial capture). Updates Invoice.amount_paid_cents += payment.amount_cents.
hooks.note: If this hook fails, the whole Payment insert rolls back — an invoice must not be marked paid without a corresponding Payment row.

hooks.name: RecordTransactionOnPaymentCaptured
hooks.purpose: Runs in the same DB transaction as the Payment insert. Dispatches TransactionRecorder.record($payment) to create the double-entry ledger movement: debit cash, credit accounts_receivable. Sets payment.transaction_id.
hooks.note: If this hook fails, the whole Payment insert rolls back — no Payment exists without its transaction record.

hooks.name: FireMarketingCaptureOnPaymentCaptured
hooks.purpose: Fires the domain event `finance.membership_purchased` when the parent invoice references a membership. The growth::marketing module's MarketingEventCapturer picks it up via #[AsDomainEventMapper] + dispatches to configured ad-network providers. Runs AFTER the atomic side effects (Invoice.mark_paid + Transaction.record) but BEFORE commit — a failure here does NOT roll back the payment (marketing capture is fail-soft; a lost signal is one wasted ad-network dispatch, not a lost payment).

hooks.name: EscalateDisputeToChargebackOnLost
hooks.purpose: Fires when a PaymentDispute transitions to status='lost'. Dispatches EscalateDisputeToChargebackJob → creates a Chargeback row via the chargeback module + sets dispute.chargeback_id. This is the bridge from the pre-chargeback dispute lifecycle to the regulator-facing chargeback lifecycle.

hooks.name: DetachPaymentMethodsOnTenantSuspended
hooks.purpose: Soft-deletes every payment_method for the suspended tenant with deactivated_reason='tenant_suspended'. Dispatches provider-side detach (best-effort). Cancels in-flight intents. Prevents captures during the suspension window.
hooks.note: Reversible — TenantResumed reactivates the methods that were deactivated by the suspension cascade (checks the audit trail for reason='tenant_suspended').

no_bootstrapper.reason: Payment does not need a tenant-boot hydration path — every operation is on-demand load from the DB. Contrast with modules like `tenancy` (bootstraps request-context) or `settings` (bootstraps config cache) that need per-request tenant hydration.
